label: NP Phase 1 Finalization Record — Diagnostic Foundations
description: Authoritative finalization record for Phase 1 (Diagnostic Foundations). Confirms: (1) slug rename applied, (2) all 8 scope fences persisted and verified, (3) Phase 1 content complete, (4) pre-Phase-2 adjustment checklist.

slugRename.reason: Original slug implied management scope (adrenal insufficiency treatment). Renamed to endocrine-crisis-differentials-recognition to correctly reflect recognition-only differential scope covering: adrenal insufficiency, thyroid storm, myxedema coma, DKA, HHS, euglycemic DKA, and hypoglycemic crisis.
slugRename.scopeFenceEntryCurrent: SF-05 (pending) will fence endocrine-crisis-differentials-recognition against adrenal-insufficiency-stress-dosing-management and thyroid-disease-diagnosis-management in Phase 2-3

scopeFencePersistenceAudit.description: All 8 slugs named in the prompt are covered across SF-01 through SF-04 in np-scope-fences.json. Additional phase1_generation_rules are also persisted in the same file.

fences.phase1Note: Neither slug appears in Phase 1. DKA/HHS questions in Phase 1 cover recognition only — no step therapy or GLP-1/SGLT-2 content generated.

Thyroid recognition questions in Phase 1 (fatigue topic: np-p1-051, np-p1-052) correctly stop at TSH/T4 recognition and do not include levothyroxine dosing, titration, drug interactions, or monitoring. COMPLIANT.
CKD anemia question (np-p1-058) references EPO deficiency as cause but does not include ESA dosing, IV iron protocols, or renal drug adjustment tables. COMPLIANT.
MDD recognition question (np-p1-056) identifies DSM-5 criteria and PHQ-9 scoring. No antidepressant agent selection, SSRI vs SNRI comparison, dosing, or titration content. COMPLIANT.

scopeFencePersistenceAudit.endocrineCrisisScopeRule: PERSISTED — endocrine-crisis-differentials-recognition owns recognition and triage disposition ONLY. Management mechanics (fluids, hydrocortisone dosing, antithyroid drug selection) are explicitly prohibited from this topic's content.

topics.shortfallNote: Add: (1) renal colic vs AAA in older male, (2) vesicoureteral reflux recognition in child with recurrent UTIs and DMSA scan finding.
Add: (1) RA synovitis vs OA using morning stiffness + RF/anti-CCP, (2) septic arthritis vs crystal arthropathy synovial fluid WBC thresholds, (3) patellofemoral pain syndrome in young female runner.

Add: (1) subconjunctival hemorrhage — benign vs serious, (2) allergic conjunctivitis vs vernal keratoconjunctivitis. Note: topic slug in readiness report is rash-differential-primary-secondary-urgent; ensure consistency with blueprint slug rash-differential-primary-care.
Add: (1) subconjunctival hemorrhage, (2) allergic conjunctivitis vs vernal keratoconjunctivitis, (3) herpes simplex keratitis with dendritic ulcer on fluorescein staining.
Add: GPA (Wegener's) presenting with hemoptysis + sinusitis + saddle nose — c-ANCA differentiation from anti-GBM disease.

Add: (1) drug-induced lymphadenopathy (phenytoin, hydralazine), (2) biopsy-timing decision (>4-6 weeks, >1cm, non-tender hard, supraclavicular), (3) toxoplasma lymphadenitis in immunocompetent patient.

topics.slugRenameNote: RENAMED from adrenal-insufficiency-thyroid-storm-recognition and endocrine-crisis-recognition-dka-hhs-adrenal. All question records updated. Scope rule persisted: recognition and triage only — no management mechanics.

duplicationReport.verdict: PASS — No duplicate stems. All 132 stem anchors are unique. All near-collision risks reviewed and resolved to distinct teaching objectives.

nearCollisionResolutions.risk: Chest pain — ACS vs thunderclap headache
nearCollisionResolutions.resolution: ACS questions focus on chest presentation, ECG patterns, and HEART score stratification. Thunderclap headache questions focus on SAH diagnosis via LP. No content overlap.
np-p1-071 tests vasovagal via prodrome and trigger; np-p1-075 tests seizure vs syncope via tonic-clonic before LOC and post-ictal state. Different anchors, no stem overlap.

nearCollisionResolutions.risk: Fever — EBV appearing in fatigue AND fever topics
nearCollisionResolutions.resolution: np-p1-057 (fatigue): confirmatory test for mono (monospot). np-p1-065 (fever): amoxicillin rash phenomenon — not a true penicillin allergy. np-p1-070 (fever): heterophile-negative mono (CMV/HIV differential). All use unique clinical scenarios with distinct teaching objectives.

nearCollisionResolutions.risk: DKA appearing in fatigue AND endocrine-crisis topics
nearCollisionResolutions.resolution: np-p1-054 (fatigue): uncontrolled T2DM as cause of fatigue — HbA1c 9.8% recognition. np-p1-127–129 (endocrine-crisis): T1DM DKA recognition (Kussmaul, anion gap), HHS differential, euglycemic DKA from SGLT-2. Entirely distinct scenarios, patients, and teaching points.

nearCollisionResolutions.risk: Thyroid content in fatigue vs endocrine crisis topics
nearCollisionResolutions.resolution: Fatigue (np-p1-051, 052): recognition of hypothyroid/hyperthyroid symptom patterns and initial TSH test. Endocrine crisis (np-p1-131, 132): thyroid storm and myxedema coma in acute decompensation. Non-overlapping: fatigue = outpatient lab recognition; endocrine crisis = acute decompensation.

nearCollisionResolutions.risk: EBV in lymphadenopathy vs fatigue/fever topics
nearCollisionResolutions.resolution: Lymphadenopathy questions (np-p1-122–126) cover Hodgkin, Virchow's node, sarcoidosis, PGL-HIV, CLL, cat scratch disease, and cervical abscess. EBV is not re-tested; each uses a unique pathology.

nearCollisionResolutions.risk: Pyelonephritis in flank pain (np-p1-084) vs fever (np-p1-069)
nearCollisionResolutions.resolution: np-p1-084 (flank pain): upper vs lower tract UTI distinction using CVA tenderness. np-p1-069 (fever): disposition decision — when to admit vs treat outpatient in a septic-appearing diabetic. Different clinical scenarios, different teaching objectives.

notes: All Phase 1 content correctly stays within the recognition and diagnostic workup boundary.

Intentional exceptions accepted: questions involving 'administer X immediately' (np-p1-001 aspirin for STEMI, np-p1-046 ceftriaxone for meningococcemia, np-p1-099 epinephrine, np-p1-130 hydrocortisone for adrenal crisis) are recognition-driven actions intrinsic to identifying the emergency — not prescribing protocols.
SF-01 (T2DM/GLP-1): no violation — T2DM content in Phase 1 (np-p1-054, np-p1-127–129) covers only recognition of uncontrolled T2DM and DKA/HHS. No step therapy or GLP-1/SGLT-2 mechanism content.
SF-02 (Thyroid): no violation — Thyroid questions test TSH/T4 interpretation and symptom recognition only. No levothyroxine dosing.
SF-03 (CKD): no violation — CKD anemia question names EPO deficiency as cause. No ESA dosing or dose adjustment tables.
SF-04 (MDD): no violation — MDD question tests DSM-5 criteria and PHQ-9 score interpretation. No antidepressant agent comparison.

requiredBeforePhase2Begins.description: Generate 14 targeted shortfall questions for 6 Phase 1 topics that are below target.
requiredBeforePhase2Begins.constraint: All shortfall questions must follow Phase 1 generation rules (recognition only, unique stem anchors, hard or expert difficulty). Topics and specific questions to add are documented in np-phase1-readiness-report.json → phase1TopicAdjustmentsNeeded.

requiredBeforePhase2Begins.description: The rash topic is referenced with two different slugs in existing files: rash-differential-primary-secondary-urgent (readiness report) vs rash-differential-primary-care (blueprint). Confirm canonical slug and apply globally before Phase 2 generation.
requiredBeforePhase2Begins.recommendation: Use rash-differential-primary-care (matches blueprint). Update readiness report reference.

requiredBeforePhase2Begins.type: New Scope Fences Required for Phase 2
requiredBeforePhase2Begins.description: Phase 2 generates prescribing content. Two additional scope fences must be defined before Phase 2 begins to prevent overlap in high-risk topic pairs.

newFencesNeeded.overlapZone: Drug adjustment decisions at follow-up visits
newFencesNeeded.separatingPrinciple: Step-therapy owns first-visit or uncontrolled-BP prescribing decisions. Follow-up owns monitoring labs, treatment response interpretation, and adherence counseling for a patient already on established therapy.

HF-GDMT owns: which drug class to add/up-titrate and when (guideline thresholds, NYHA class decisions). ACE-ARB-ARNI owns: drug-specific mechanism, adverse effects (cough, angioedema, hyperkalemia), contraindications, monitoring parameters.

requiredBeforePhase2Begins.description: np-question-bank-expansion-plan.json uses slightly different slugs for some Phase 1 topics (e.g., 'flank-pain-differential-uti-stones-pyelo', 'rash-differential-primary-care', 'red-eye-differential-conjunctivitis-glaucoma'). Align these with the canonical Phase 1 slugs before the expansion plan is used for question generation.
affectedSlugsInExpansionPlan: flank-pain-differential-uti-stones-pyelo → should match phase1 canonical slug
lymphadenopathy-reactive-malignant-workup → should match phase1 canonical slug

canProceedToPhase2Without: ADJ-04 (low priority, expansion plan sync can trail Phase 2 generation)

phase1QuestionIndex.description: Complete stem anchor registry for all 132 Phase 1 questions. Used to verify uniqueness when generating Phase 2 questions that involve adjacent clinical scenarios.

phase2ExecutionOrder: 1. Define SF-05 and SF-06 fence rules (ADJ-03)
2. Fill Phase 1 shortfall questions (ADJ-01) — 14 questions across 6 topics
3. Begin Phase 2 with Prescribing domain: antibiotic-stewardship-outpatient, ace-arb-arni-indications-monitoring, doac-selection-monitoring-reversal
4. Follow with Chronic Disease: hypertension-step-therapy-guideline, heart-failure-gdmt-primary-care, afib-rate-rhythm-stroke-risk
5. Defer GLP-1/SGLT-2 and T2DM intensification until SF-01 has been re-validated against Phase 2 content
